county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Moore County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Dumas. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Dumas. North Panhandle county organized 1892. Moore County Historical Museum. Farming and cattle feedlots. Dumas area oil and gas. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Moore County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Dumas holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Moore County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1876 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Moore County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1876. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Moore County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1876. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Moore County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1876 to 1950 for Moore County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Moore County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Moore County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Morris County formed 1875 from Titus. County seat is Daingerfield. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Daingerfield. Northeast Texas county. Morris County Historical Museum. Daingerfield State Park. Check Titus for pre-1875 records. Iron furnace heritage (19th century iron smelting). Lake o' the Pines area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Morris County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1875; County Clerk in Daingerfield holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Motley County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Matador. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Matador. Rolling Plains county organized 1891. Motley County Historical Commission. Matador Ranch territory (Scottish-owned, 1879-1951). Ranch records are significant. Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Motley County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Matador holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- Motley County Area (Comanche and Kiowa)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Comanche and Kiowa in the Motley County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Motley County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Motley County from 1876. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nacogdoches County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Nacogdoches). County seat is Nacogdoches. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Nacogdoches. Original Texas county — the oldest town in Texas (Spanish mission 1716). Stone Fort Museum at Stephen F. Austin State University. Sterne-Hoya House Museum. SFA East Texas Research Center is a major repository. El Camino Real heritage. Caddo Indian records. Spanish colonial records. Nacogdoches Archives at TSLAC. Exceptionally rich early documentation.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Nacogdoches County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Nacogdoches holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Nacogdoches County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Nacogdoches County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Nacogdoches County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Nacogdoches County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Nacogdoches County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- Nacogdoches County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. Nacogdoches County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Nacogdoches County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Nacogdoches County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Nacogdoches County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Nacogdoches County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Navarro County formed 1846 from Robertson. County seat is Corsicana. North Texas / Blackland Prairie region with farming and urban growth heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Corsicana. Central Texas county. Pioneer Village in Corsicana. Navarro County Historical Society. First major Texas oil discovery at Corsicana (1894). Check Robertson for pre-1846 records. Significant African American community. Czech heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Navarro County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Corsicana holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Navarro County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Navarro County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Corsicana
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1894. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Navarro County from 1846. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Newton. Deep East Texas county (Sabine River border). Newton County Historical Commission. Timber heritage. Check Jasper for pre-1846 records. Significant African American community. Big Thicket area.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nolan County formed 1876 from Bexar, Young. County seat is Sweetwater. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sweetwater. West-central Texas county organized 1881. Pioneer City-County Museum. Sweetwater Rattlesnake Roundup heritage. Check Bexar and Young for pre-organization records. Ranching and farming.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Nolan County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Sweetwater holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Nueces County formed 1846 from San Patricio. County seat is Corpus Christi. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Corpus Christi. South Texas Gulf Coast county. Corpus Christi Museum of Science and History. Mary and Jeff Bell Library at Texas A&M University-Corpus Christi. Naval Air Station Corpus Christi records at NARA. Significant Mexican-American heritage. Check San Patricio for pre-1846 records. King Ranch territory (partial).
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Nueces County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1846; County Clerk in Corpus Christi holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Oldham County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Vega. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Vega. Northwest Panhandle county organized 1880. Oldham County Historical Commission. Cal Farley's Boys Ranch heritage. Tascosa (ghost town) was the 'Cowboy Capital of the Plains.' Check Bexar for pre-organization records. XIT Ranch territory.
